title: Project with mixed VHDL and Verilog generate wrong cell for PLL
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue
A [repro is available](https://github.com/infphyny/yosys-mixed-hdl-issue) for lattice ecp5 device. Also an example from [fomu workshop](https://github.com/im-tomu/fomu-workshop/tree/master/hdl/mixed/blink) have a similar problem. 

This issue is reproductible for version of yosys after commit  cdb57118758f855518ad416d12728d72bff58c10 
The project implement a barrel shifter in vhdl and use a pll in verilog generated by ecppll program.

## Expected behavior

In the mixed hdl repro cited above, Yosys after the version mentioned generate wrong PLL cell and nextpnr-ecp5 generate error : 
```
cell type 'ECP5PLL' is unsupported 
``` 
ECP5PLL is the name of the module given to ecppll program that generate the verilog file pll.v in my case . The cell that yosys should synthetize is EHXPLLL instead of ECP5PLL. 

Good yosys output for nextpnr-ecp5:
```
=== top ===

   Number of wires:               1298
   Number of wire bits:           1904
   Number of public wires:        1298
   Number of public wire bits:    1904
   Number of memories:               0
   Number of memory bits:            0
   Number of processes:              0
   Number of cells:               1252
     EHXPLLL                         1
     L6MUX21                       202
     LUT4                          691
     PFUMX                         326
     TRELLIS_FF                     32

```

Yosys output that generate error for nextpnr-ecp5
```
=== top ===

   Number of wires:               1295
   Number of wire bits:           1912
   Number of public wires:        1295
   Number of public wire bits:    1912
   Number of memories:               0
   Number of memory bits:            0
   Number of processes:              0
   Number of cells:               1254
     ECP5PLL                         1
     L6MUX21                       203
     LUT4                          693
     PFUMX                         325
     TRELLIS_FF                     32

```



Thanks.
